page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The instance is configured with 256 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in multi-threaded workloads and memory operations. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 67,610.19 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 17,690.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4,103.87 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 122.41 GB/sec. It demonstrates top-tier performance in static web serving throughput at 86.82 GB/sec. Additionally, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate workloads like cryptography and low-precision integer operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-64 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a tradeoff for IOPS-heavy databases or large-scale deep learning training. The instance is highly suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing for 7B parameter models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, running on an Intel x86_64 architecture with 32 physical cores. It features a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and a 260 MB L3 cache, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 67,610.19 and top-tier static web serving throughput of 86.82 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for cryptography and matrix operations. This resource profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for balanced workloads, making it highly suitable for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, Redis caching, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel x86_64 architecture, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and Intel x86_64 architecture for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring Intel AMX instructions, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM on Intel x86_64 architecture with 32 physical cores
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix operations
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier static web serving throughput of 86.82 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference
